summary: Improved sysdolphin baselib light-object (`lobj`) matching by replacing several inline assembly implementations with C, factoring repeated reference-count/release logic into `HSD_LObjUnrefThis`, and factoring current-light-list reset logic into small inline helpers. The PR also converted `HSD_LObjGetType` from a header macro to a real C function and adjusted small expression details such as removing explicit `u8` casts in color animation assignments and dropping an unused `HSD_LObjSetup` parameter. Evidence is strongest for decomp/matching cleanup and asm removal; there were no review comments explaining reviewer intent.
what_changed: Added `u32 HSD_LObjGetType(HSD_LObj* lobj)` in `lobj.c`, replacing the previous `#define HSD_LObjGetType(o) (o->flags & 0x3)` macro in `lobj.h`.
Changed `LObjUpdateFunc` light color channel assignments from explicit casts like `(u8)(255.0 * val->fv)` to direct assignments `255.0 * val->fv`, likely to better match compiler conversion behavior.

Changed `HSD_LObjSetup` signature from `void HSD_LObjSetup(HSD_LObj *lobj, GXColor color, f32 shininess, u32 unused)` to `void HSD_LObjSetup(HSD_LObj *lobj, GXColor color, f32 shininess)`, removing an unused argument.
Added `HSD_LObjUnrefThis(HSD_LObj* lobj)` to centralize the `ref_DEC` / release / destroy sequence previously duplicated inline in multiple functions.
Replaced duplicated manual reference decrement and virtual method release/destroy code in `HSD_LObjDeleteCurrent` and `HSD_LObjRemoveAll` with calls to `HSD_LObjUnrefThis`.
Replaced large inline assembly implementations for `HSD_LObjDeleteCurrentAll`, `HSD_LObjSetCurrentAll`, and `func_803668EC` with C implementations.
Introduced inline helper `LObjRemoveAll()` to clear `active_lights`, reset `nb_active_lights`, unref all objects in `current_lights`, and remove list nodes with `HSD_SListRemove`.
Introduced inline helper `LObjReplaceAll(HSD_LObj* lobj)` to perform the same current-light reset and then add each light in an input linked list via `HSD_LObjAddCurrent`.

Implemented `HSD_LObjDeleteCurrentAll(HSD_LObj* lobj)` in C with two paths: if `lobj` is non-null, iterate through the linked list and delete each current light; otherwise clear all current lights via `LObjRemoveAll`.
Implemented `HSD_LObjSetCurrentAll(HSD_LObj* lobj)` in C by calling `LObjRemoveAll()` then `HSD_LObjAddCurrent(lobj)`.
Implemented `func_803668EC(HSD_LObj* lobj)` in C by calling `LObjReplaceAll(lobj)`, retaining an `unused` local seen in the diff likely for matching purposes.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated object unref/release/destroy sequence into `HSD_LObjUnrefThis`, which reduced large duplicated compiler-sensitive code blocks and made the surrounding list-manipulation functions easier to match and maintain.
Used `inline` helper functions (`LObjRemoveAll`, `LObjReplaceAll`) to preserve repeated generated-code patterns while avoiding three separate hand-written assembly bodies.

Matched former assembly behavior by keeping the two distinct reset flows: one helper only clears current lights, while the replacement helper clears then re-adds every node in the supplied `HSD_LObj` linked list.
Preserved null-check behavior in `HSD_LObjUnrefThis` and callers, including the redundant-looking second `lobj != NULL` check after `ref_DEC(lobj)`, which may help reproduce original control flow.
Removed explicit casts in color assignments, recognizing that source-level casts can alter generated conversion instructions even when the destination field is an 8-bit color channel.
Kept apparently unused locals such as `u32 unused;` in new matched C wrappers, suggesting a deliberate stack/register-shaping tactic rather than purely cosmetic cleanup.
decomp_lessons: When several inline asm functions differ only in the final action, first extract the common generated-code sequence into an inline helper; this can unlock multiple matches while reducing copied code.

For HSD object lifetime code, prefer using shared helpers like `ref_DEC` plus `HSD_OBJECT_METHOD(...)->release/destroy` instead of manually re-expanding refcount logic in every caller, if the helper still matches.
Header macros that hide field operations may need to become real functions when the original binary has a callable symbol or when a function body improves matching elsewhere.
Do not assume explicit C casts improve matching; in this PR, assigning a floating expression directly to `GXColor` byte fields was used instead of explicit `(u8)` casts.

Unused parameters and locals are matching-sensitive. Removing an unused function parameter (`HSD_LObjSetup`) or preserving an unused local (`unused`) can both be valid depending on what the target code requires.

For current light state, the canonical reset sequence shown here is: clear all `active_lights` entries up to `GX_MAX_LIGHT`, set `nb_active_lights = 0`, then drain `current_lights` with `HSD_LObjUnrefThis(current_lights->data)` and `HSD_SListRemove`.
Linked-list operations should cache `next` before deleting/unreffing the current object when the current node/object may be destroyed, as shown in `HSD_LObjRemoveAll`.
naming_conventions: Public HSD light-object APIs use the `HSD_LObj...` prefix, e.g. `HSD_LObjGetType`, `HSD_LObjUnrefThis`, `HSD_LObjDeleteCurrentAll`, `HSD_LObjSetCurrentAll`, `HSD_LObjRemoveAll`.
Private/static or file-local helpers use `LObj...` without the `HSD_` prefix, e.g. `LObjUpdateFunc`, `LObjRemoveAll`, `LObjReplaceAll`.
Light-object variables are consistently named `lobj`; traversal temporaries use short names such as `cur` and `next`.
Global/current light state names use descriptive snake_case: `active_lights`, `current_lights`, `nb_active_lights`, `lightmask_specular`.
Animation constants retain existing HSD attribute naming such as `HSD_A_L_LITC_R`, `HSD_A_L_LITC_G`, `HSD_A_L_LITC_B`, and `HSD_A_L_LITC_A`.
The diff preserves an unknown function name `func_803668EC`, indicating no stronger semantic name was introduced in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed inline asm for `HSD_LObjDeleteCurrentAll`, `HSD_LObjSetCurrentAll`, and `func_803668EC`, replacing them with matched C wrappers plus inline helpers.
Used `inline void LObjRemoveAll()` and `inline void LObjReplaceAll(HSD_LObj* lobj)` to reproduce repeated reset/drain code patterns previously visible in assembly.
Centralized the refcount decrement/release/destroy sequence through `HSD_LObjUnrefThis`; this turns verbose manual refcount code into a helper call while preserving behavior through `ref_DEC`.
Direct assignment from float expression to `u8` color channels was used instead of explicit casts, a small source expression change likely aimed at instruction-level matching.
The new C replacements retain small matching artifacts such as `u32 unused;` locals in `HSD_LObjSetCurrentAll` and `func_803668EC`.
The old asm showed unrolled/looped zeroing of `active_lights`; the C replacement expresses it as a `for (i = 0; i < GX_MAX_LIGHT; i++) active_lights[i] = NULL;`, relying on compiler output to match.
The PR body explicitly says it matched some inline asm functions and reduced code duplication using inlines, which aligns with the diff evidence.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the supplied PR slice.
One review is counted, but the excerpt contains no review body or actionable feedback.
The PR body states: "Match some inline asm functions" and "Reduce code duplication using inlines"; no further rationale was provided.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching `src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c` to see whether `func_803668EC` was eventually named semantically.
Search for other uses of `HSD_LObjSetup` to verify all call sites were updated after removing the unused parameter.
Compare later `HSD_LObjUnrefThis` usage across baselib to see whether this helper became the preferred object lifetime idiom.
Search for similar macro-to-function conversions in HSD headers where callable symbols were needed for matching.
Investigate whether the `u32 unused;` locals in `HSD_LObjSetCurrentAll` and `func_803668EC` remained necessary or were later removed after compiler/profile changes.
